name: Produce social posts with Airtable and WordPress at scale

messageValues.message: You write production-quality engagement content. Follow the brief exactly, match the requested tone, and return clean markdown with a title line. No preamble, no explanations.
You are a strict editor. Check the draft for factual gaps, tone mismatches and weak openings. Return the improved draft only — do not describe your changes.

parameters.text: =✅ Produce social posts with Airtable and WordPress at scale — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 9:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Stamp & Shape Post

### Stamp & Shape Post

Shapes the item: writes `postId`, `receivedAt`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Payload Valid?

### Payload Valid?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.postId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Build the Brief · ②→ Record Rejected Input

### Record Rejected Input

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Build the Brief

Shapes the item: writes `brief`, `tone`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Draft with AI Writer

### Draft with AI Writer

Single fixed LLM step (no tool choice) — cheaper and more predictable than an agent.

Ports: ①→ Refine with AI Editor · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### OpenRouter Writer Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Draft with AI Writer** as `languageModel`

🔑 OpenRouter API key

### Refine with AI Editor

Single fixed LLM step (no tool choice) — cheaper and more predictable than an agent.

Ports: ①→ Convert to HTML · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### OpenRouter Editor Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Refine with AI Editor** as `languageModel`

🔑 OpenRouter API key

### Convert to HTML

Converts markdown to HTML for rich rendering in email/CMS.

Next: Publish Draft to WordPress

### Publish Draft to WordPress

Publishes as a WordPress **draft** post, never live — a human reviews and hits Publish, keeping AI-authored content from going out unsupervised.

Ports: ①→ Serialize Run Output (CSV) · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 WordPress credentials

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow

Delegates to a reusable sub-workflow (single-responsibility building block) and waits for its result.

Ports: ①→ Record Sub-workflow Result · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Record Sub-workflow Result

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Pulls every page from the Notion database — fine for small datasets; add a filter if the database grows large.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per post (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Guard Non-Empty content

### Guard Non-Empty content

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Notion)

### Append Audit Row (Notion)

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#social`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#social`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Notion integration token

# Produce social posts with Airtable and WordPress at scale

**Pattern:** Content Generation · **Level:** Architect · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Produce social posts with Airtable and WordPress at scale. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual engagement is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Stamp & Shape Post → Payload Valid? → Build the Brief → Draft with AI Writer → Refine with AI Editor → Convert to HTML → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to content generation without manual effort
- Anyone automating engagement in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Notion integration token; OpenRouter API key; Slack OAuth2; WordPress credentials.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Two LLM calls, not one, because a model grading its own output rarely catches its own mistakes — a dedicated critic pass, with a different system prompt focused on gaps and tone rather than generation, catches what a single writing pass misses. Publishing lands as a **draft**, never live, so a human stays the final gate before AI-authored content goes public.

## Practice exercises

About as deep as this pattern goes — extend it:

1. Swap the Editor Model to a different provider than the Writer Model and compare the critique quality.
2. Change the critic's system message to focus on one specific weakness, like SEO or tone.
3. Add a third pass — a fact-check chain — between the writer and the editor.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your engagement process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.

json.content: Behind the scenes: how we build workflow templates
